entries.title: Old Republic Is Not Prequel Republic

content.fact: The Old Republic should not be written with the exact political, military, and Jedi structure of the late prequel Republic.
content.injection: Keep Old Republic institutions era-appropriate: ancient, war-tested, and often more openly involved in Jedi/Sith conflicts than the prequel-era status quo.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Old Republic.
Respect story-position bounds from Old Republic and Jedi Foundations to Ruusan Reformation and Old Republic End-State.
antiLore: Do not import clone troopers, Palpatine-era Senate dynamics, or Order 66 assumptions into Old Republic scenes.
Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

continuity.sourceBoundary: Legends Old Republic material covering Tales of the Jedi, Knights of the Old Republic, KOTOR II, SWTOR-adjacent eras, ancient Sith/Jedi state, and late Old Republic transition context before the prequel-era Republic.
continuity.notes: Development deck. Keep separate from Disney canon Old Republic reintroductions and from later film-era context unless stacked intentionally.

sourceInfo.title: Old Republic

entries.title: Old Republic Sith Can Be Public

content.fact: In many Old Republic contexts, Sith are public military, political, or academy actors rather than hidden Rule-of-Two conspirators.
content.injection: When the relevant Context supports it, Sith can openly command fleets, attend academies, hold titles, and rule territory.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Old Republic.
Respect story-position bounds from Old Sith Empire Ascendant to Eternal Empire Crisis.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

entries.title: Jedi Romance Rules Vary by Era and Character

content.fact: Attachment norms, enforcement, and personal practice vary across Old Republic sources and eras.
content.injection: Do not apply one rigid film-era romance rule to every Old Republic Jedi; match the active source window and character situation.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Old Republic.
Respect story-position bounds from Old Republic and Jedi Foundations to Ruusan Reformation and Old Republic End-State.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Hyperdrive and Maps Still Control Plot

content.fact: Even with advanced travel, hyperspace routes, maps, blockades, and unknown regions can still control plot access.
content.injection: Use hyperspace constraints to preserve stakes: ancient routes, navicomputer data, blockades, and unmapped space can matter.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Old Republic.
Respect story-position bounds from Old Republic and Jedi Foundations to Ruusan Reformation and Old Republic End-State.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

content.fact: KOTOR and SWTOR material contains route/player-choice ambiguity that should not be flattened without user-selected assumptions.
content.injection: If a story depends on route outcomes, keep the deck conservative or ask the user’s Context/route rather than assuming every optional choice happened.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: KOTOR/SWTOR Route Caution.

entries.title: Old Republic Future Film State Guard

content.fact: Film-era facts are millennia in the future for most Old Republic play and should not bleed backward.
content.injection: Do not reference Anakin, Luke, Palpatine’s Empire, the Clone Wars, the Galactic Civil War, or New Republic outcomes unless the story is time-travel/crossover-aware.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Old Republic.
Respect story-position bounds from Old Republic and Jedi Foundations to Ruusan Reformation and Old Republic End-State.
antiLore: Block Skywalker saga leakage in normal Old Republic scenes.
Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Bane Rule of Two Is Late Guard

content.fact: Darth Bane’s Rule of Two belongs to the late Old Republic/Ruusan transition, not the earlier Exar Kun, Revan, or SWTOR contexts.
content.injection: Before the late New Sith Wars/Ruusan Context, do not treat the Rule of Two as active Sith doctrine. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Late New Sith Wars.
Respect story-position bounds from Late New Sith Wars to Ruusan Reformation and Old Republic End-State.
antiLore: Do not retroactively impose Bane’s doctrine on ancient Sith or KOTOR-era Sith.
Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

content.injection: Do not reveal Revanite/Eternal Empire/Valkorion material during early SWTOR class-era roleplay unless active Context has advanced to those arcs.

entries.title: Old Republic Does Not Have Stormtroopers

content.fact: Old Republic militaries should not use film-era stormtrooper or clone-trooper assumptions by default.
content.injection: Use Republic soldiers, Sith troopers, Mandalorians, droids, militia, or era-appropriate forces instead of stormtroopers/clones unless a crossover explains it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Old Republic.
Respect story-position bounds from Old Republic and Jedi Foundations to Ruusan Reformation and Old Republic End-State.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Force Techniques Should Match Training Source

content.fact: Old Republic Force powers should match the user’s era, order, teacher, and source rather than being universal menu options.
content.injection: When granting Force abilities, check training lineage and era; rare powers, alchemy, Battle Meditation, and wound-related effects need specific justification.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Old Republic.
Respect story-position bounds from Old Republic and Jedi Foundations to Ruusan Reformation and Old Republic End-State.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.
